fast_tests: cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --lib: ok. 2 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored
search_limit_production_env_read: none -- removed in fix-1; phase-2 rerank LIMIT defaults to SEARCH_RERANK_LIMIT (10) and is overridable ONLY via the test setter Engine::set_search_limit_for_test, clamped >=10 so it can never shrink below production semantics
fix1: codex BLOCK remediation (additive). The prior EU7_SEARCH_LIMIT std::env::var read in read_search_in_tx was an ambient-env override compiled into the production hot path (a runtime with EU7_SEARCH_LIMIT>=10 would change real search fanout), so the earlier search_limit_is_noop_confirmed=true was an overclaim. Replaced it with the canonical repo test-seam pattern (mirrors set_mean_recompute_dynamic_max_for_test / AtomicU64 on ProjectionRuntimeShared): added search_limit_override: AtomicUsize (init SEARCH_RERANK_LIMIT=10) to ProjectionRuntimeShared, removed the env read entirely, search_inner loads the atomic (clamped >=10) and threads it into ReaderRequest::Search, and added pub Engine::set_search_limit_for_test(usize). The recall harness (eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs measure_recall) now calls engine.set_search_limit_for_test(10+slack) instead of relying on EU7_SEARCH_LIMIT; all EU7_SEARCH_LIMIT usage removed from src/ and the harness. Chose the test-setter over a cargo feature because it matches existing repo precedent exactly and production already contains NO env read (default behavior identical to the original hardcoded LIMIT 10). Verified: cargo build (engine+embedder+cli) clean; cargo build --tests --features default-embedder (eu7_real_corpus_ac + eu8_ir_validation) clean; cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --lib ok (2 passed); grep confirms no EU7_SEARCH_LIMIT and no env read for the search limit in production code or the harness.
notes: CORRECTED post-fix-1 (orchestrator, 2026-05-31, codex CONCERN #1): head_sha now points at the fix-1 commit 5b69568 and the stale EU7_SEARCH_LIMIT no-op claim below is SUPERSEDED by the fix1 field above -- there is no longer any env read in production; the prior lib.rs:3169 env-seam was removed. [Historical, pre-fix-1:] All three cherry-picks (a601d1c, 07f1a76, 5658d39) applied with ZERO conflicts (textual auto-merge on lib.rs + eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs for a601d1c; corpus_subset.rs applied cleanly with no conflict so no additive resolution was needed). GT-embed logging is test-only, added to the doc_vecs build loop in eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs: a GT_EMBED_START n=<n> marker before the loop + a throttled (30s Instant cadence, mirroring EU7_SEED_PROGRESS) EU7_GT_EMBED_PROGRESS embedded=<i>/<n> rate_docs_per_s=<r> line inside; gated to the GT-embed phase only, no engine behavior change. Did NOT run AGENT_LONG real-corpus recall or EU-8 full-corpus (needs weights + ~45min). cargo build (engine+embedder+cli) clean; cargo build --tests --features default-embedder clean; all three test targets compile (eu7/eu8 in engine crate, candle_dump in embedder crate). The GT-embed commit passed the cargo fmt --check pre-commit hook. The output.json commit follows this one.
